title: The Old Survey
content:
آ آ آ آ Our moneyï؟½s all spent, to the deuce went it!
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The landlord, he looks glum,
آ آ آ آ On the tap-room wall, in a very bad scrawl,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ He has chalked to us a sum.
آ آ آ آ But a glass weï؟½ll take, ere the grey dawn break,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And then saddle up and awayï؟½
آ آ آ آ Theodolite-tum, theodolite-ti, theodolite-too-ral-ay.

آ آ آ آ With a measured beat fall our horsesï؟½ feet,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Galloping side by side;
آ آ آ آ When the moneyï؟½s done, and weï؟½ve had our fun,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ We all are bound to ride.
آ آ آ آ Oï؟½er the far-off plain weï؟½ll drag the chain,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And mark the settlerï؟½s wayï؟½
آ آ آ آ Theodolite-tum, theodolite-ti, theodolite-too-ral-ay.

آ آ آ آ Weï؟½ll range from the creeks to the mountain peaks,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And traverse far below;
آ آ آ آ Where foot never trod, weï؟½ll mark with a rod
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The limits of endless snow;

آ آ آ آ Each lofty crag weï؟½ll plant with a flag,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ To flash in the sunï؟½s bright rayï؟½
آ آ آ آ Theodolite-tum, theodolite-ti, theodolite-too-ral-ay.

آ آ آ آ Till with cash hard-earned once more returned,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ At ï؟½The Beaverï؟½ bars weï؟½ll shout;
آ آ آ آ And the very bad scrawl thatï؟½s against the wall
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Ourselves shall see wiped out.
آ آ آ آ Such were the ways in the good old days!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ The days of the old survey!
آ آ آ آ Theodolite-tum, theodolite-ti, theodolite-too-ral-ay.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
